# Problem Statement

Our V0 deep dive ([Blending analysis](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1LnVdfh2_xdswdgB1Gly04kqfn7-fh0uviQ9MqFuzGHo/edit?tab=t.0)) revealed a couple of interrelated friction points. For V1 efforts, we plan to focus on the following three Gaps:

1. Certain NV Carousel Impression Drops → Negative OR Impacts
    

- The top 3 carousels drove ~90% of the lift, while mid-to-long tail NV carousels saw a ~39% drop in impressions, especially for carousels like Grocery. 
    
- While the lift of top carousels is massive, the drop of some carousels still yield negative NR OR lifts (such as Grocery -8%, BIA -6%).
    

![](data:image/png;base64,)

2. Top-of-Funnel NV Awareness Drops
    

- With V0 Blender the NV impressions from HP drops by 8%. LCM and discovery-oriented modules lost visibility.
    
- Bugs on LCM Carousel boosting, some of the impression drops are coming from mistakenly categorized LCM carousels as Rx, which leads to lower ranking than expected. 
    
- As a result, we traded long-term diversity and upper-funnel NV awareness for short-term D7 conversion efficiency.
    

|   |   |   |   |   |   |   |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
|Top Contributors to NV OR Lift|   |   |   |   |   |   |
|CAROUSEL|Lift contrib %|NV Orders (rel)|Impressions (rel.)|Pos change|C: Order mix %|T: Order mix %|
|Quick essentials nearby|37%|156%|131%|-3.78|3%|7%|
|Convenience & drugstores|27%|64%|19%|-0.78|6%|8%|
|Recently viewed|25%|32%|33%|-0.93|10%|12%|
|Local liquor stores|5%|75%|57%|-4.96|1%|1%|
|Top pickup options nearby|5%|99%|156%|-4.36|1%|1%|
|Saved stores|3%|24%|39%|-1.2|2%|2%|
|Convenience & grocery|2%|62%|20%|-0.84|1%|1%|
|Top finds for you|2%|60%|44%|-2.02|1%|1%|
|Late night cravings|2%|43%|54%|-1.55|1%|1%|
|Top Detractors to NV OR Lift|   |   |   |   |   |   |
|Grocery|-8%|-16%|-25%|1.66|6%|5%|
|Buy it again|-6%|-43%|-36%|2.42|2%|1%|
|Deals for you|-1%|-42%|-17%|2.22|0%|0%|
|Midnight munchies|0%|-83%|-68%|10.59|0%|0%|
|Ready-to-Drink|0%|-70%|-76%|15.24|0%|0%|
|Ice Cream|0%|-85%|-72%|14.15|0%|0%|

  

3. Infrequent Cohort Degradation
    

- While Active users thrived, HP CVR dropped for New (-3.6%), Resurrected (-2%), and Very Churned (-4.7%) segments.
    
- The underlying rankers rely too heavily on historical engagement. Without that history, infrequent users faced a "cold start" penalty, missing out on the high-converting utility carousels they needed to see.
    

![](data:image/png;base64,)

# Scope

Goals

- Simple enhancement of the intent model
    
- Simple iteration of the FIV weights of NV business verticals. 
    

  

Non-Goals

- Full revamp of the intent model
    
- Complicated intent model development
    

# Design

For V1 iteration, we plan to focus on following 3 areas:

- Bug Fix – LCM Categorization: Correct misclassification of LCM carousels to ensure they receive proper NV intent treatment and restore unintended exposure loss.
    
- Cx Intent Model Upgrade: Evolve the static intent scalar into a session-aware model incorporating lifecycle cohort and post-checkout context signals.
    
- BVID Weight Tuning: Calibrate vertical level weights (FIV weights) to rebalance exposure, reduce over concentration, and recover mid-/long-tail NV visibility under guardrails.
    
- Offline Simulation: an offline simulation engine to help us select the most promising candidates efficiently. 
    

  
  

LCM Blending Bug Fix 

[Frank Zhang](mailto:frank.zhang@doordash.com)

  

### Iterating the Cx Intent Model

The current intent model relies on simple inputs (business vertical, hour of day) + simple linear formulation. In V1, we will evolve it to become Cohort &Contextual-Aware with lightweight tree models.

|   |   |   |   |
|---|---|---|---|
|Dimension|V0 (Current State)|V1 - Proposal|Why It Matters|
|Features|Hour day part and Business Vertical|Add <br><br>1. lifecycle cohort <br>    <br>2. session-level contextual signals (e.g., post-checkout state)|Enables differentiation between cold-start sessions and exploration windows|
|Model Form|Linear Piecewise Form|Explore:<br><br>- Linear with Lifecycle interaction<br>    <br>- Lightweight Tree Model (LGB Tree)|Captures non-linear and interaction effects (cohort × context)|
|Model Training Label||Label current HP sessions to 4 classes:<br><br>- Rx Only<br>    <br>- NV Only<br>    <br>- Both (Rx +NV)<br>    <br>- Non converted<br>    <br><br>  <br><br>Then build multiclass model to predict the intent||
|Model Usage||Use relative strength between Rx/NV to fine tune NV vs. Rx weights.||

  

### Tuning the Business Vertical Weights (FIV weight)

As a stepping stone to the ML team's full Future Incremental Value (FIV) model, V1 will introduce a refined set of business vertical weights ( to calibrate the intent scores.

|   |   |
|---|---|
|Options|Description|
|FIV based Index|If FIV by vertical is available, we can use Rx as baseline and build index for each verticals, such as Rx =1, Grocery =3, etc. instead of current heuristic based weights|
|Defer to UR Raw Scores|If FIV by vertical is not available yet, we propose to revert to UR predictions which ensure we have the relevancy quality.|

  

Offline Simulation

One key pain point in blending development is the lack of a clearly defined optimization objective or evaluation framework. For example, we may have 10 different candidate ways to combine the intent score to reweight NV vs. Rx, but running A/B tests for all variants is slow, expensive, and operationally inefficient.

To address this, we propose to formalize an offline evaluation framework as a structured pre-screening layer before online experimentation. We will leverage the existing UR evaluation pipeline to simulate candidate parameter configurations prior to any online exposure.

Importantly, we recognize that offline signals are not direct quantitative predictors of online metrics. Instead, offline evaluation will serve to:

- Validate directional consistency of metric movement
    
- Establish guardrails (e.g., exposure floors, share bounds) to prevent extreme shifts
    
- Measure average ranking position changes across cohorts and segments
    
- Analyze top-K composition and domain balance to bound exposure risk
    

By introducing this offline evaluation layer, we aim to significantly accelerate iteration velocity, narrow down viable candidates before A/B testing, and reduce experimentation cost while maintaining risk control.

  

Example: Intent Model + Blender Iteration

![](data:image/png;base64,)

The diagram illustrates our proposed offline evaluation framework for blending iteration. Multiple Cx intent model candidates (e.g., Candidate A and B) can be combined with different blender configurations to form several intent–blender variants. Each variant is then replayed on the same historical HP score logs through an offline simulation layer. For a high level, the simulation will have the following steps. 

- Prepare inputs: select intent model candidates (A, B, ...) and the baseline HP score logs (control traffic).
    
- Generate variants: combine each intent candidate with one or more blender configurations (mapping, index buckets, clamps) to produce intent–blender variants.
    
- Simulation/Replay traffic: run each variant through the offline simulation using the same historical HP logs so all candidates share a consistent baseline.
    
- Compute standardized metrics: for each variant produce the same evaluation outputs (pHomepage MRR,  NV vs Rx MRR, Cx cohort-level MRR, impression deltas, top-K composition, etc.).
    
-  Shortlist for online test: pick top-performing, guardrail-compliant variants for targeted A/B testing (1–2 candidates), reducing online experimentation cost.
    

This ensures that all candidates are evaluated under a consistent traffic baseline. The simulation outputs standardized evaluation results across predefined objectives and guardrails (e.g., overall ranking quality, NV-specific performance, and cohort-level stability).

Based on these offline results, we can shortlist the most promising configurations for online A/B testing, significantly reducing experimentation cost and accelerating iteration velocity while maintaining risk control.

# ML Development Plan

|   |   |   |   |   |
|---|---|---|---|---|
|Task|Description|Eng effort|Timeline|Status|
|RFC and Review||1w|Feb 27, 2026|In Progress|
|Offline Simulation Development|Refactor the UR evaluation process as a comprehensive simulation engine for blender|1w|Feb 27, 2026|Not Started|
|BIZV Weight Tuning|Tuning the BIZV weight (removing, etc.)|1w|Mar 6, 2026|Not Started|
|Cx Intent Model Enhancement|Build a simple Cx intent model|1w|Mar 6, 2026|Not Started|
|Model Simulation + Selection|Combine BIZV weight + Cx intent for offline simulation and model selections.|1w|Mar 13, 2026|Not Started|
|BE Changes (Bug fixes, SPS calls)|Fixes the LCM + SPS calls (if doable)|1w|Mar 13, 2026|Not Started|
|A/B Testing|Test party, A/B Testing setup|0.5-1w|Mar 18, 2026|Not Started|
